betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Flare Stack§f

§aBurn off excess flammable fluids and recover a portion of their energy — essential for keeping oil extraction running!§f

The Flare Stack is a disposal machine that eliminates excess flammable or gaseous fluids, typically byproducts from oil drilling and refining. It recovers a small amount of power from combustion, making it useful for getting rid of unwanted products while reclaiming some energy.

§6Crafting§f:
Crafted in the §bAssembly Factory§f using §e12× Steel Ingot§f, §e12× Iron Ingot§f, §e4× Copper Plate§f, §e1× Steel Tank§f, §e8× Steel Pipe§f, §e4× Steel Shell§f, and §e3× Thermoelectric Element§f.

§6Operation§f:
Supply the flare stack with a fluid and configure it via the §bflow control valve§f and §bignition switch§f.
§b• Gas Venting§f: Turn on the flow control valve to vent non-flammable gases at §e50mB/t§f — no power generated.
§b• Combustion§f: Turn on both the flow control valve and ignition switch to burn flammable liquids/gases.
§b• Liquid Combustion§f: Generates §e10%§f of the fluid's combustion energy, consuming §e10mB/t§f.
§b• Gas Combustion§f: Generates §e20%§f of the fluid's combustion energy, consuming §e10mB/t§f.
§b• Speed Upgrades§f: Each tier increases consumption by §e10mB/t§f.

§6Comparison§f:
Compared to the §bDrainage Pipe§f, the flare stack is slower but can reclaim marginal power from energy-dense fluids like §bKerosene§f.

§eTip§f: The §bNatural Gas§f byproduct from the derrick is better stored and cracked later, but if your gas buffers are full, the flare stack prevents extraction from stalling.
